domains.title: My Domains
domains.description: Claim a domain you control. After MX records point to AnyMail's email worker, you can create mailboxes under it.
domains.deleteConfirm: Remove {{name}}? You won't be able to receive mail at this domain anymore.
domains.emptyHint: Add one above to create mailboxes under it
domains.setupStep1: Add this domain to a Cloudflare account with Email Routing enabled.
domains.setupStep2: Set its MX records to Cloudflare's: route1.mx.cloudflare.net, route2.mx.cloudflare.net, route3.mx.cloudflare.net (priority 10/20/30).
domains.setupStep3: Configure a catch-all rule routing to the AnyMail email worker; ask the AnyMail admin if you don't host it yourself.
domains.sharedDescription: Use addresses on AnyMail's shared domain. Mail arrives automatically — no MX changes required.
domains.sharedNotConfigured: The admin has not configured a shared inbox domain yet. Once they do, your personal addresses will appear here.
domains.suffixLabel: Personal suffix — any address ending in your token belongs to you
domains.suffixHint: Use a fresh local-part per service to know who leaked your address. Example: {{example}}
domains.relayLabel: Forward target — forward mail from any external address into your AnyMail inbox
domains.relayHint: On your existing email host (Cloudflare/Google/etc.), set a catch-all forward to this address. AnyMail will recover the original recipient from the message headers.

login.title: Sign in to AnyMail
login.description: Enter your email and password to continue

register.title: Create an account
register.description: Sign up to manage your mailboxes
register.haveAccount: Already have an account? Sign in

errors.passwordShort: Password must be at least 6 characters

receive.title: Code Receiver
receive.formDescription: Paste an API key (with emails:read scope) to fetch emails from the last {{minutes}} minutes. No login required.
receive.idle: Enter an API key and click Fetch to start.
receive.noEmails: No emails in the last 10 minutes.

inbox.title: Inbox
inbox.noEmailsHint: Emails will appear here once received

compose.title: Compose Email
compose.subject: Subject
compose.required: From, To, and Subject are required

accounts.title: Accounts

tags.newGroupBulkDesc: Create a new group and move the {{count}} selected account(s) into it.
tags.newGroupRowDesc: Create a new group and move this account into it.
tags.deleteGroupConfirm: Delete group "{{name}}"? Accounts in this group will be moved back to Untagged.
tags.hint: Group accounts with a tag (e.g. "used"). Leave empty for no tag.
tags.selectAllHint: Select accounts to group or move

accounts.removeConfirm: Delete account {{email}} and all its emails?

dialog.title: Add Account
dialog.description: Choose account type to create or import

domain.description: Create an email address on your domain (requires Cloudflare Email Routing)
domain.invalidEmail: Please enter a full email address, e.g. user@yourdomain.com

oauth.description: Connect your Gmail or Outlook account via OAuth

import.description: Import Outlook / Hotmail / Live accounts in bulk. One per line:

groups.title: Groups
groups.description: Manage account groups in one place: create, rename, delete, or jump to the accounts in each group.
groups.newDesc: Create an empty group. You can move accounts into it from the Accounts page or here.
groups.mergeWarn: "{{name}}" already exists — saving will merge into that group.
groups.deleteConfirmWithAccounts: Delete group "{{name}}"? The {{count}} account(s) inside will be moved back to Untagged.
groups.emptyHint: Click "New group" to start organizing your accounts

settings.title: Settings
settings.cloudflareDescription: Domain sync and one-click Email Routing (needs Zone:Read + Email Routing Edit)
settings.domainsDescription: Select which domains can be used for creating email accounts
settings.domainsEmpty: No domains yet. Use the import wizard above, or add manually / sync from Cloudflare.
settings.domainsHint: {{count}} domain(s) enabled. Click to toggle, x to remove.
settings.domainImportDescription: Follow the MX guide, verify DNS, then enable the domain
settings.domainImportedForced: Force-imported {{domain}} (MX not ready; mail may fail)
settings.autoEnableHint: Import auto-enables Email Routing and catch-all → Worker (zone must be on this CF account; configure API Token / Account ID).

autoEnableErrors.zone_not_found: Zone not found in this Cloudflare account (point NS here first)
autoEnableErrors.get_routing_failed: Failed to read Email Routing status
autoEnableErrors.enable_routing_failed: Failed to enable Email Routing (check token permissions)

settings.mxGuideIntro: Route domain mail via Cloudflare Email Routing to this Worker to receive mail.
settings.mxStep1: Add the domain to your Cloudflare account (or use a zone already on Cloudflare).
settings.mxStep2: Open Email → Email Routing and enable it (Cloudflare will create MX records).
settings.mxStep3: If DNS is hosted elsewhere, add the required MX records listed below.
settings.mxStep4: In Email Routing → Routing rules, add catch-all (or *@domain) → Send to a Worker → any-mail.
settings.mxStep5: Click Check MX to verify public DNS, then Import & enable.
settings.mxGuideNote: Full Email Routing usually requires Cloudflare nameservers. MX propagation can take minutes to 48 hours. After enabling, create mailboxes on the Accounts page.
settings.mxCheckOk: MX points to Cloudflare Email Routing
settings.mxNotReady: MX is not ready. Configure DNS using the guide before importing.

mxStatus.mx_ok: MX fully matches Cloudflare Email Routing.
mxStatus.mx_ok_with_extra: Cloudflare MX found, but other MX records exist (may affect routing).
mxStatus.mx_partial: Some Cloudflare MX records found; add all three for best results.
mxStatus.mx_empty: No MX records yet. Add them first.
mxStatus.mx_not_cloudflare: MX does not point to Cloudflare Email Routing.
mxStatus.dns_error: DNS lookup failed. Try again later.

apiKeys.title: API Keys
apiKeys.description: For external program access (e.g. code reception). Keys are shown only once at creation — save them securely.
apiKeys.name: Name
apiKeys.addressHint: Restrict this key to emails sent to a matching address (substring match). Leave empty for no restriction.
apiKeys.editDescription: Update name, scopes, account type, or expiry. The key value itself is unchanged.
apiKeys.revokeConfirm: Revoke key {{name}}? This cannot be undone.
apiKeys.emptyHint: Click "Create Key" to create one for external use
apiKeys.plaintextWarning: Copy this key now. You won't be able to see it again after closing.
